questions.text: EDR quarantines a signed driver loading from a temp folder. Next BEST step?

options.text: Isolate host, collect timeline, and check for lateral movement indicators
Restore driver because it is signed, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Disable EDR to stop alerts, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Reimage without any logging, without security or identity team coordination per policy.

questions.explanation: Signed but anomalous drivers can be malicious; investigate before release.
questions.concept: Investigate signed-but-anomalous binaries and restrict unapproved software installation.

questions.text: Users download 'free PDF tools' from ads. Which control reduces malware installs?

options.text: Block all internet indefinitely, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Disable Windows Defender, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Allow admin rights for all staff, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Application allowlisting and software center for approved tools

questions.explanation: Allowlisting limits unsigned or unapproved installers common in droppers.
questions.concept: Investigate signed-but-anomalous binaries and restrict unapproved software installation.

questions.text: A Word doc with macros calls out to a paste site. Classification?

options.text: Network beaconing only, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Physical tailgating, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Benign marketing material, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Malware delivery attempting staged download

questions.explanation: Macro-to-external-download is classic malware staging.
questions.concept: Investigate signed-but-anomalous binaries and restrict unapproved software installation.

questions.text: EDR detects a living-off-the-land script spawning rundll32 from WinWord. Next step?

options.text: Reboot once and close the ticket if the user can open documents again, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Isolate the host, collect triage package, and hunt for lateral movement from that user
Whitelist rundll32 because it is a signed Microsoft binary on all systems, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Add a firewall rule blocking Word globally without examining the endpoint, without security or identity team coordination per policy.

questions.explanation: Office spawning rundll32 is suspicious; isolate and investigate before declaring clean.
questions.concept: Investigate signed-but-anomalous binaries and restrict unapproved software installation.

questions.text: File servers show mass .encrypted extensions and a ransom note. FIRST action?

options.text: Pay ransom immediately without documentation, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Reboot all domain controllers first, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Delete backups to free space, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Isolate affected segments and preserve volatile evidence per IR plan

questions.explanation: Containment and evidence preservation precede recovery decisions.
questions.concept: Isolate, preserve evidence, and restore from clean backups before negotiating payment.

questions.text: Backups are immutable; leadership asks about paying ransom. Security recommendation?

options.text: Disable MFA to speed recovery, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Publish decryption keys online, using shared credentials instead of individual accountability.
Restore from immutable backups and involve legal/law enforcement; payment does not guarantee decryption
Pay anonymously on personal card, without security or identity team coordination per policy.

questions.explanation: Immutable backups are primary recovery; payment is uncertain and may fund crime.
questions.concept: Isolate, preserve evidence, and restore from clean backups before negotiating payment.

questions.text: Ransomware group threatens leak of stolen data. What does 'double extortion' imply?

options.text: They encrypt twice with different algorithms only, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Backups are automatically safe from leak, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
They may publish exfiltrated data even if you restore from backups
They only attack printers, without security or identity team coordination per policy.

questions.explanation: Exfiltration plus encryption means recovery does not eliminate leak risk.
questions.concept: Isolate, preserve evidence, and restore from clean backups before negotiating payment.

questions.text: A subsidiary reports encrypted file shares but still has network connectivity. IMMEDIATE action?

options.text: Segment affected VLANs and disable compromised service accounts per runbook
Pay a small ransom immediately to test whether attackers provide working keys
Announce full recovery on social media to reassure customers during investigation, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Restore from the newest online snapshot without verifying backup integrity first, without security or identity team coordination per policy.

questions.explanation: Segmentation limits spread; preserve evidence before mass restore attempts.
questions.concept: Isolate, preserve evidence, and restore from clean backups before negotiating payment.

questions.text: An analyst sees powershell.exe spawning rundll32 with no window. What should they collect FIRST?

options.text: Printer queue status, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Parent/child process tree, command line, and hash of loaded DLL
User's last vacation dates, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Only CPU fan speed, without security or identity team coordination per policy.

questions.explanation: Process lineage and command lines are core to endpoint investigation.
questions.concept: Parent/child process chains reveal LOLBins, injection, and suspicious network children.

questions.text: Task Manager shows a browser consuming CPU but Process Explorer shows no matching process. Likely explanation?

options.text: User imagination, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Normal GPU acceleration, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Process hollowing or rootkit-style hiding—use EDR kernel telemetry
Patch Tuesday side effect only, without security or identity team coordination per policy.

questions.explanation: Discrepancies suggest advanced evasion; EDR kernel view is needed.
questions.concept: Parent/child process chains reveal LOLBins, injection, and suspicious network children.

questions.text: Which artifact BEST helps determine persistence via scheduled task creation?

options.text: DHCP lease log only, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
CCTV timestamp, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Windows Security Event ID 4698 and Task Scheduler operational log
Invoice PDF metadata, without security or identity team coordination per policy.

questions.explanation: Task creation events document persistence mechanisms.
questions.concept: Parent/child process chains reveal LOLBins, injection, and suspicious network children.

questions.text: Sysmon shows powershell.exe -enc launching from excel.exe with outbound 443. Meaning?

options.text: Proof that TLS inspection is misconfigured on the perimeter firewall only
Normal Excel behavior when opening any spreadsheet from email attachments, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Indicates a printer driver update scheduled by Windows Update automatically, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Likely macro-driven execution downloading or executing a second-stage payload

questions.explanation: Encoded PowerShell from Office is a classic malware staging pattern.
questions.concept: Parent/child process chains reveal LOLBins, injection, and suspicious network children.

questions.text: Registry Run keys point to a random AppData executable after an email attachment. BEST remediation?

options.text: Remove persistence entries, quarantine binary, and reimage if tampering is widespread
Ignore if antivirus is green, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Delete only the email, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Disable Run keys globally forever, without security or identity team coordination per policy.

questions.explanation: Persistence must be removed with the payload; reimage if integrity is uncertain.
questions.concept: Startup items, scheduled tasks, and services maintain access after reboot.

questions.text: A WMI event subscription recreates malware after reboot. Why is this effective persistence?

options.text: WMI only manages printers, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
WMI subscriptions can trigger payloads without classic Run key visibility
It encrypts backups automatically, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
It blocks MFA enrollment, without security or identity team coordination per policy.

questions.explanation: WMI persistence is stealthy; hunters search for suspicious consumers.
questions.concept: Startup items, scheduled tasks, and services maintain access after reboot.

questions.text: Startup folder contains a shortcut to a remote SMB path. Risk?

options.text: Only affects Linux servers, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Shortcuts are always harmless, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Improves patch compliance, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Logon triggers load of code from attacker-controlled share

questions.explanation: Remote startup items pull code at user logon—common persistence.
questions.concept: Startup items, scheduled tasks, and services maintain access after reboot.

questions.text: Autoruns shows an unknown scheduled task calling a binary in %ProgramData%. Action?

options.text: Delete only the task file so the binary remains for future malware research, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Reboot the PC once without collecting logs to clear persistence automatically, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Disable the task, quarantine the binary, and search for other hosts with same hash
Ignore if the binary is larger than one megabyte because large files are safe

questions.explanation: Unknown scheduled tasks with unusual paths warrant hunt and remediation.
questions.concept: Startup items, scheduled tasks, and services maintain access after reboot.

questions.text: 20% of endpoints report outdated definitions for 14 days. BEST prioritized action?

options.text: Disable updates during business hours permanently, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Exclude all servers from scanning, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Identify update failures (proxy, service stopped) and enforce compliance via MDM
Uninstall antivirus to improve performance, without security or identity team coordination per policy.

questions.explanation: Stale defs reduce detection; fix root cause of update failures.
questions.concept: AV/EDR must stay enabled, updated, and centrally monitored—not disabled for convenience.

questions.text: A server excludes the entire C: drive from scanning for 'performance.' What risk acceptance is this?

options.text: AV scans cause guaranteed data loss, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Malware can reside undetected on excluded volumes
Exclusions apply only to network traffic, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Exclusions improve encryption strength, without security or identity team coordination per policy.

questions.explanation: Broad exclusions create blind spots attackers target.
questions.concept: AV/EDR must stay enabled, updated, and centrally monitored—not disabled for convenience.

questions.text: EDR shows 'protected' but Windows Defender is disabled via GPO conflict. What should you do?

options.text: Disable logging to reduce tickets, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Reconcile GPO and EDR policy so one compliant agent is active and reporting
Leave both disabled if users complain, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Hide status in the console, without security or identity team coordination per policy.

questions.explanation: Conflicting policies cause false confidence; one managed agent should report health.
questions.concept: AV/EDR must stay enabled, updated, and centrally monitored—not disabled for convenience.

questions.text: GPO audit shows 18% of laptops with Defender disabled by local admin tools. Fix?

options.text: Allow users to disable AV during presentations without logging events, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Whitelist all unsigned drivers to prevent future Defender alerts, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Remove AV entirely on those laptops to improve boot time for sales staff
Enforce tamper protection via policy and investigate who disabled protection

questions.explanation: Disabled endpoint protection is a critical finding; restore centrally managed AV/EDR.
questions.concept: AV/EDR must stay enabled, updated, and centrally monitored—not disabled for convenience.
